steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: An equation has no solution when the variable cancels and leaves a false statement (a contradiction like $-3 = 3$). The left side is $3(x-1) = 3x - 3$ in every choice, so we need the right side to simplify to $3x + c$ where $c ≠ -3$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$-3(-x+1)$': expands to $3x - 3$, so $3x - 3 = 3x - 3$ — true for all $x$ (infinite solutions). ✗
  • Choice B — '$-3(-x-1)$': expands to $3x + 3$, so $3x - 3 = 3x + 3$ — gives $-3 = 3$, a contradiction. ✓
  • Choice C — '$4(x-1)$': expands to $4x - 4$, so $-x = -1$, $x = 1$ (one solution). ✗
  • Choice D — '$4(-x+1)$': expands to $-4x + 4$, so $7x = 7$, $x = 1$ (one solution). ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
